task: I’m trying to decide whether to buy a used truck I found, but I don’t want to jump in and regret it later. Can you help me make a simple go/no-go checklist for what I should verify before I spend the money?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple **go / no-go used truck checklist** you can use before buying, with an emphasis on the stuff that saves you from expensive surprises.

## 1) Paperwork first
**GO if:**
- Clean title matches VIN
- No salvage / rebuilt / flood history unless you knowingly want that
- Maintenance records exist
- Mileage makes sense for the age and condition

**NO-GO if:**
- Title problems, missing title, or VIN mismatch
- Flood, salvage, or major accident history you weren’t expecting
- Seller can’t explain ownership or history clearly

## 2) Cold start check
Try to see it **cold**, not already warmed up.

**GO if:**
- Starts quickly
- Idles smoothly
- No heavy smoke after startup
- No loud knocking, grinding, or major rattles

**NO-GO if:**
- Hard start when cold
- Excessive white/blue smoke
- Knocking, ticking, or metal sounds that don’t go away

## 3) Basic fluid check
Look for obvious signs of neglect.

**GO if:**
- Oil, coolant, transmission fluid, and brake fluid look reasonably clean
- No milkshake/oil-coolant contamination
- No wet leaks all over the engine, trans, or underbody

**NO-GO if:**
- Coolant in oil or oil in coolant
- Burning smell plus leaks
- Major active leaks from engine, transmission, or rear end

## 4) Battery / charging / grounds
Since you care about electrical issues, check this early.

**GO if:**
- Battery terminals are clean and tight
- Truck starts without obvious strain
- Charging voltage looks normal if you can test it
- No hacked wiring or lots of electrical “fixes”

**NO-GO if:**
- Corroded or loose battery connections
- Repeated jump starts needed
- Signs of bad grounds, melted wiring, or electrical gremlins

## 5) Scan for codes
Even if the truck “runs fine,” scan it.

**GO if:**
- No active codes, or only minor historical codes with a good explanation
- Readiness monitors and live data look normal

**NO-GO if:**
- Multiple current codes
- Misfire, transmission, sensor, or communication codes with no clear cause
- Seller clears codes right before showing it to you

## 6) Road test
Drive it long enough to warm up.

**GO if:**
- Shifts smoothly
- Tracks straight
- Brakes straight and firm
- No overheating
- No vibration, clunking, or steering looseness

**NO-GO if:**
- Transmission slips, bangs, hunts, or delays
- Steering wander, death wobble, or severe vibration
- Overheats, hesitates, or loses power under load

## 7) Underbody / rust check
This can make or break the deal.

**GO if:**
- Frame, mounts, brake lines, and suspension are solid
- Normal surface rust only
- No obvious structural rot

**NO-GO if:**
- Frame rust, soft spots, patchwork repairs, or crushed mounts
- Brake line corrosion
- Rust so bad it’s becoming a safety or repair issue

## 8) Listen for “big-ticket” signs
These usually mean walk away or price it very low.

**NO-GO if:**
- Engine knock
- Transmission slip
- Coolant loss with no obvious leak
- Heavy blow-by
- Severe smoking
- 4x4 issues if you need 4x4
- ABS/brake warning lights that won’t clear

## 9) Seller behavior
This matters more than people think.

**GO if:**
- Seller is straightforward
- Answers questions directly
- Lets you inspect and test it properly

**NO-GO if:**
- Rushes you
- Won’t let it cool down for a cold start
- Won’t allow a scan or inspection
- Gives vague answers or changes the story

## Simple final rule
**GO** if the truck has:
- Clean paperwork
- No major rust
- No major leaks
- Good cold start
- No serious codes
- Normal road test
- No signs of engine, transmission, or electrical trouble

**NO-GO** if you find:
- Title issues
- Structural rust
- Overheating
- Transmission problems
- Engine knock
- Electrical problems that look like a bigger system issue

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page printable checklist** with checkboxes and a **“walk away / negotiate / buy”** score at the bottom.

user_simulator.text: Pretty good. But make it tighter and more practical — like something I can actually carry around and use in the lot. Add a hard stop for frame rust and cooling problems, and maybe a quick note on maintenance records vs. just a clean-looking engine. Also, I’d want a line about verifying 4x4 if it’s supposed to have it. Keep it one page, checkboxes, no extra chatter.

turns.agent_response:
## Used Truck Go / No-Go Checklist

**Truck:** ____________________  **VIN:** ____________________  **Date:** ____________________

### 1) Paperwork
- [ ] Title matches VIN
- [ ] Seller name matches title / can legally sell it
- [ ] No salvage / rebuilt / flood history unless expected
- [ ] Mileage makes sense for age and condition

**GO** if all above check out  
**NO-GO** if title is missing, VIN doesn’t match, or history is unclear

---

### 2) Maintenance / Condition
- [ ] Maintenance records available
- [ ] Records show regular oil changes / basic service
- [ ] Clean engine bay does **not** replace records
- [ ] No signs of major neglect, leaks, or fresh cleanup hiding problems

**GO** if records support the condition  
**NO-GO** if seller only has a “clean-looking” engine but no proof of care

---

### 3) Cold Start
- [ ] Starts cold without long cranking
- [ ] Idles smoothly
- [ ] No knocking, grinding, or heavy ticking
- [ ] No blue / white smoke after startup

**GO** if cold start is clean  
**NO-GO** if it’s hard to start, smokes, or makes bad noises

---

### 4) Cooling System — HARD STOP
- [ ] Coolant level looks normal
- [ ] No overheating during idle or test drive
- [ ] No coolant smell, bubbling, or pressure issues
- [ ] No leaks at radiator, hoses, water pump, or heater core

**HARD STOP / NO-GO** if it overheats, loses coolant, or shows signs of cooling system failure

---

### 5) Frame / Rust — HARD STOP
- [ ] Frame is solid
- [ ] No soft spots, holes, cracks, or heavy scaling
- [ ] Brake lines and mounts are not badly rusted
- [ ] Cab / bed mounts look intact

**HARD STOP / NO-GO** if the frame has serious rust, rot, or structural damage

---

### 6) Electrical / Battery
- [ ] Battery terminals are clean and tight
- [ ] Starts without obvious strain
- [ ] No warning lights staying on
- [ ] No hacked wiring or obvious electrical repairs

**GO** if electrical system looks normal  
**NO-GO** if it needs jumps, has bad grounds, or has electrical gremlins

---

### 7) 4x4 Verification
- [ ] If equipped, 4x4 engages correctly
- [ ] 4HI / 4LO work as expected
- [ ] No grinding, binding, or flashing 4x4 lights
- [ ] Front axle / transfer case operation seems normal

**GO** if 4x4 works properly  
**NO-GO** if it won’t engage, binds, or has warning lights/issues

---

### 8) Scan / Codes
- [ ] No active codes
- [ ] No recent code clearing right before the sale
- [ ] No misfire / trans / ABS / communication codes

**GO** if scan is clean or only minor explained history  
**NO-GO** if there are current serious codes

---

### 9) Road Test
- [ ] Shifts smoothly
- [ ] Brakes straight and firm
- [ ] Tracks straight
- [ ] No vibration, clunks, or steering looseness
- [ ] No overheating under load

**GO** if it drives normally  
**NO-GO** if it slips, wanders, shakes, or overheats

---

## Final Call
**BUY** only if:
- [ ] Paperwork is clean
- [ ] Maintenance proof is reasonable
- [ ] No frame rust
- [ ] No cooling problems
- [ ] 4x4 works if equipped
- [ ] No major codes
- [ ] Road test is solid

**WALK AWAY** if any hard stop item fails.

user_simulator.text: That works. Tight enough to use in the lot, and the hard stops are where they should be. The maintenance records note is the part a lot of people skip — clean engine bay doesn’t mean much by itself. Good.
